generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACDamperActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical damper actuator for HVAC systems, modeled and configured within BIM for remote control via Digital Twin. Supports standardized control protocols (e.g., BACnet, Modbus) for interoperability with Building Control Systems."
]

instance LightingSystemActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical lighting system actuator enabling dynamic control of lighting levels based on occupancy and environmental conditions. Integrated with BIM for spatial and operational synchronization with the Digital Twin."
]

instance ValveControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical valve actuator for water/fluid systems, configured within BIM to enable remote operation through Digital Twin-mediated commands. Implements safety constraints during runtime synchronization."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-frequency temperature sensor integrated into BIM for real-time monitoring of environmental conditions. Supports sub-second sampling for critical HVAC performance synchronization with the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureSensorDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy detection sensor embedded in BIM to monitor space utilization. Provides real-time data for dynamic control logic and energy optimization within the Digital Twin framework."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancySensorDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy consumption meter integrated into BIM for real-time energy monitoring. Enables data-driven insights for predictive maintenance and optimization services within the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyMeterDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACPerformanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized sensor for monitoring HVAC system performance metrics (e.g., airflow, efficiency). Provides high-frequency data for real-time synchronization with the Digital Twin's control logic."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACPerformanceSensorDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Real-time temperature data transmitted via low-latency protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) from physical sensors to the Digital Twin for synchronization."
]

instance OccupancySensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
    base:desc "Occupancy status data transmitted in real-time to the Digital Twin for dynamic control adjustments and energy optimization."
]

instance EnergyMeterDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyMeter
    base:desc "Energy consumption data streamed to the Digital Twin for real-time monitoring and predictive analytics."
]

instance HVACPerformanceSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACPerformanceSensor
    base:desc "High-frequency HVAC performance metrics transmitted via OPC UA for real-time synchronization with the Digital Twin's control systems."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMEnrichedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM model integrating static geometric data with dynamic operational metadata (e.g., sensor/actuator mappings, control logic). Serves as the foundational semantic layer for Digital Twin deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMEnrichmentLayer, AutomatedTransformationEngine
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable control logic model derived from BIM-enriched models, defining PID controllers and rule-based logic for Building Control Systems. Automatically generated via Model-Driven Engineering transformations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AutomatedTransformationEngine, SimulationEngine
]

instance RealTimeBuildingStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time data from physical building systems (e.g., sensor readings, actuator states) synchronized bidirectionally with the Digital Twin. Enriched with BIM metadata for contextual interpretation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureSensorDataTransmitted, OccupancySensorDataTransmitted, EnergyMeterDataTransmitted, HVACPerformanceSensorDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataHarmonizationLayer, RealTimeVisualizationService
]

instance DeviceConfigurationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Static device specifications (e.g., sensor/actuator capabilities, spatial relationships) extracted from BIM models. Used for validation and configuration of Digital Twin components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AutomatedTransformationEngine, DeviceModelingService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMEnrichmentLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering layer extending static BIM models with dynamic operational metadata (e.g., sensor mappings, control logic). Bridges the gap between geometric representations and real-time Digital Twin requirements."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, ControlLogicImplementationService
]

instance AutomatedTransformationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MDE-driven transformation engine using tools like ATL or QVT to extract structured representations from BIM models and generate deployable Digital Twin artifacts (e.g., OPC UA configurations, REST APIs)."
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedArtifactGenerationService, SimulationEngine
]

instance DataHarmonizationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Ontology-based layer ensuring semantic consistency between heterogeneous data sources (e.g., BIM, IoT sensors, ERP systems) via schema validation rules and standardized mappings."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance RealTimeCommunicationProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Bidirectional synchronization protocols (OPC UA, MQTT/CoAP) enabling low-latency, high-frequency data exchange between physical devices and the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ClosedLoopControlService
]

instance VisualizationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D/4D rendering engine (e.g., Unity3D, Unreal Engine) for immersive visualization of building operations with real-time data overlays (e.g., temperature maps, occupancy heatmaps)."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeVisualizationService, ScenarioSimulationService
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulator (e.g., EnergyPlus, IES VE) integrated with the Digital Twin for validating control logic and predicting energy consumption under varying conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance AIAnalyticsModule : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning and AI module (e.g., Python/R-based analytics with scikit-learn, TensorFlow) embedded within the Digital Twin for predictive maintenance, anomaly detection, and adaptive control."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance CustomServiceSDK : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software Development Kit providing standardized interfaces (RESTful APIs, WebSocket streams) for DT engineers to develop and integrate custom plugins or microservices."
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomPluginDevelopmentService
]

// C6: Services
instance DeviceModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling Building Control Engineers to model sensors, actuators, and controllers directly within BIM, creating a unified semantic foundation for Digital Twin artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:provides SemanticDeviceDefinitionInsight
]

instance ControlLogicImplementationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service supporting the definition of control algorithms (e.g., PID controllers) linked to physical devices, ensuring bidirectional synchronization between BIM and Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlLogicValidationInsight
]

instance AutomatedArtifactGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering service automatically generating deployable Digital Twin components (e.g., simulation models, API endpoints) from enriched BIM models with minimal manual intervention."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArtifactGenerationAutomationAction
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service facilitating bidirectional, real-time synchronization between physical building systems and the Digital Twin via structured data pipelines with low-latency updates."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateVisualizationInsight
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D visualization interface for real-time monitoring of building operations, system health, and anomaly detection using BIM-integrated dashboards."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveMonitoringInsight
]

instance OperationalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling remote operation of physical infrastructure (e.g., HVAC adjustments) through Digital Twin-mediated commands with audit trails for facility management."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlAction
]

instance CustomPluginDevelopmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Modular extension framework offering standardized interfaces for integrating third-party services (e.g., predictive maintenance, energy optimization) via plugins or microservices."
    DTDFVocab:provides PluginIntegrationInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven service providing predictive failure analysis, energy consumption optimization, and dynamic reconfiguration based on real-time sensor data and machine learning models."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveFailureInsight, EnergyOptimizationInsight
]

instance ClosedLoopControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling automated feedback loops between Digital Twin and physical systems, where control decisions are derived from real-time simulations or optimization models."
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomatedControlAction
]

instance ScenarioSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "What-if analysis tool testing the impact of control logic changes or infrastructure modifications before physical deployment."
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance SemanticDeviceDefinitionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Unified semantic foundation for Digital Twin artifacts created through BIM-integrated device modeling, enabling consistent representation of sensors, actuators, and controllers."
]

instance ControlLogicValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of control algorithms (e.g., PID controllers) to ensure bidirectional synchronization between BIM and Digital Twin representations."
]

instance ArtifactGenerationAutomationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of deployable Digital Twin components from enriched BIM models, reducing manual intervention and ensuring reproducibility."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of building system states, operational data, and performance metrics for situational awareness."
]

instance ImmersiveMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D visualization of building operations with real-time data overlays (e.g., temperature maps, occupancy heatmaps) for facility managers."
]

instance RemoteControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote operation of physical infrastructure (e.g., HVAC, lighting) via Digital Twin-mediated commands with audit trails."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PluginIntegrationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Integration of third-party services (e.g., predictive maintenance, energy optimization) via standardized extension points in the Digital Twin framework."
]

instance PredictiveFailureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis of equipment failures using historical sensor data and machine learning models to preempt maintenance needs."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time energy consumption optimization through demand-response algorithms and adaptive control logic."
]

instance AutomatedControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated feedback loops between Digital Twin and physical systems for real-time control adjustments based on simulation or optimization models."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ScenarioAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of potential impacts from control logic changes or infrastructure modifications before physical implementation."
]

// C1: Environment
instance BuildingControlSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical and digital environment encompassing Building Control Systems (BCS) integrated with BIM models, sensors, actuators, and Digital Twin infrastructure."
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, OccupancySensor, EnergyMeter, HVACPerformanceSensor,
                 HVACDamperActuator, LightingSystemActuator, ValveControlActuator,
                 BIMEnrichedModel, ControlLogicModel
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system involves **control flows** initiated by **Facility Managers** or **DT Engineers** through the deployed DT interface. Command types include **real-time monitoring directives, operational adjustments, and device control signals**, formatted as structured messages compliant with the framework’s **modular architecture**. Validation and authentication mechanisms are implied to be embedded within the **runtime configuration management** system, ensuring secure command execution. Feedback systems provide **bidirectional confirmation** of command execution, while **conflict resolution** is managed through the framework’s **holistic life cycle management** approach, which coordinates roles (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers) and system components. The framework’s **standardized extension points** allow for dynamic integration of validation logic, ensuring commands are authenticated and executed only after confirmation."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in the AECO domain is designed to orchestrate **cyber-physical integration** through a **systematic architecture** that combines **static models** (e.g., BIM, CAD, or simulation models) with **runtime data** (e.g., IoT sensor feeds, operational logs, or environmental metrics) to create a **unified virtual representation** of physical assets. The orchestration relies on **carefully designed integration patterns**, though the document does not specify exact **architecture patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven, or service-oriented). **Resource management** is implied to be critical due to the **complex and heterogeneous** nature of infrastructure environments, requiring **efficient data fusion, conflict resolution, and real-time analytics** to support predictive maintenance, optimization, and decision-making. **System-wide coordination mechanisms** are necessary to ensure **consistency across lifecycle stages** (design to operation), but the document lacks details on **specific protocols, middleware, or governance models** for this coordination."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** directly within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with **semantic representations** of Building Control System (BCS) components (e.g., functional attributes, connectivity, and control logic). This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts **structured representations** from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., executable models, configuration files) with **minimal manual intervention**. This ensures alignment between physical and digital representations.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed to the DT environment, enabling **real-time synchronization** between the physical building and its digital twin via **continuous data exchange capabilities**.
  4. **Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to **monitor system states**, visualize operational data through **immersive interfaces**, and **control physical infrastructure** via the twin. This phase emphasizes **interactive visualization** for real-time decision-making.
  5. **Custom Service Integration**: DT Engineers extend functionality by developing and integrating **custom services and plugins** through standardized extension points in the framework’s **modular architecture**, allowing the DT to evolve with operational needs.
  **Transition Management**: The framework supports seamless transitions between phases via **automated model transformations** and **modular deployment pipelines**, ensuring consistency across the lifecycle. **Lifecycle Governance** is implied through role-based access (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and **version control** for DT artifacts, though explicit governance frameworks (e.g., ISO standards) are not detailed in the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **model fidelity** by addressing BIM’s static limitations through **dynamic behavioral and analytical models**, ensuring alignment with physical system states. **Validation and verification** are implicit in the **real-time synchronization** requirement, where discrepancies between physical and digital systems trigger corrective actions (e.g., recalibration of control logic). **Uncertainty sources** include sensor inaccuracies, model simplifications, and latency in data transmission, though **quantification methods** are not specified. **Error detection** relies on **runtime monitoring** by **Facility Managers**, while **correction mechanisms** are automated via the framework’s **modular architecture** (e.g., plugin-based analytics). **Quality assurance** is embedded in the **life-cycle deployment process**, where **DT Engineers** extend functionality to mitigate fidelity gaps, and **Building Control Engineers** validate device configurations against operational requirements."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this DT system is designed to support **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, with a **modular architecture** enabling seamless integration of custom services. While specific protocols are not detailed, the framework prioritizes **low-latency, high-bandwidth communication** to facilitate real-time data exchange between physical and digital components. The network topology is implied to be **hybrid**, accommodating cloud, edge, or on-premise deployment models, with **redundancy mechanisms** ensuring reliability. Security protocols are aligned with the **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, emphasizing **accessibility for users with varying technical expertise** and **low-overhead maintainability**, though exact encryption or authentication methods are unspecified."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this DT system supports **multi-model hosting**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise platforms**, tailored to the operational needs of the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain. Computational and storage requirements are optimized for **scalability**, from individual components to entire infrastructure systems, with **runtime configuration management** enabling dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. The framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** facilitate **scalable deployment models**, while **availability and reliability** are assured through the non-functional requirements (NF_X), which address **maintainability and interoperability**. The deployment process is initiated by **Building Control Engineers** using BIM tools, with **Facility Managers** interacting with the deployed DT for real-time operations."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system in the AECO domain must achieve **horizontal integration** with **external systems** to address the **fragmented nature of models and data**, leveraging **interoperability standards** (e.g., IFC for BIM, OPC UA for IoT, or Industry 4.0 protocols) to enable seamless **data exchange** between the DT and **enterprise systems, legacy infrastructure, or other Digital Twins**. Dependencies on external systems are **critical**, as the DT’s value proposition hinges on **real-time, cross-domain insights** (e.g., linking building performance data with energy grids, supply chains, or regulatory databases). Integration patterns may include **API-based connectivity, event-driven architectures, or federated data lakes**, though the document does not specify exact **protocols, technologies, or latency requirements**. The DT’s ability to **collaborate with stakeholders** (e.g., architects, operators, regulators) across the **building lifecycle** implies a need for **standardized interfaces** and **semantic interoperability** to avoid silos, but no concrete examples (e.g., specific APIs, ontologies, or middleware) are provided."
]
